title: Schedule C: Profit or Loss From Business (Sole Proprietorship)
content: Schedule C is filed with Form 1040 by sole proprietors and single-member LLCs to report income or loss from a business they operated. The form calculates net profit or loss by subtracting business expenses (such as advertising, supplies, home office, vehicle use, and depreciation) from gross receipts. The resulting net profit is subject to both income tax and self-employment tax. Filers must maintain adequate records to substantiate all reported income and deductions in case of an IRS audit.
